pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 44, 49: survey created with api('add_survey', [session, 0, 'Household Financial Wellness 2025', 'en', 'G'])
setup_task.sh line 70: question created with title 'Q_EXPENSES' and type 'K' (Multiple Numerical Input)
setup_task.sh line 57-64: question created with title 'Q_INCOME' and type 'N' (Numerical Input)

Verified in three places: (1) task.json description requires 'Total expenses cannot exceed'; (2) verifier.py line 82 checks for 'cannot exceed' in tip.lower(); (3) export_result.sh line 26 retrieves validation tip from database

verifier.py lines 66 and 74-75: checks for 'sum' or '+' for summation; checks valid_operators = ['<=', '<', 'le', 'lt']. Task description line 5 explicitly uses 'sum(Q_EXPENSES) <= Q_INCOME', which includes '<=' operator not in metadata list.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic LimeSurvey titled 'Household Financial Wellness 2025' created programmatically during setup. The survey contains two core questions: Q_INCOME (type N, numerical input) and Q_EXPENSES (type K, multiple numerical input) with five expense category subquestions (Rent/Mortgage, Food/Groceries, Utilities, Healthcare, Transportation). The agent must configure Q_EXPENSES validation to require the sum of expenses not exceed Q_INCOME, with a validation tip containing 'cannot exceed' and dynamically referencing income via {Q_INCOME}. The verifier accepts validation equations using summation operators (sum or +) and comparison operators (<=, <, le, lt), though the task description explicitly specifies <= in the example equation.
